channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Netanyahu caught between the US, Lebanon war, and Iran ceasefire
item.description: As elections near, Israel's PM finds himself squeezed between his voters, his allies, and his opponents.

item.title: Apache down, fighting up: What the latest US-Iran attacks mean
item.description: Analysts say Iran seeks to deter US patrols on the Strait of Hormuz by attacking its assets across the Gulf.

item.title: Satellite images show Israel’s destruction of historical city of Tyre
item.description: Satellite data shows damage to residential areas, UNESCO Heritage buffer zones and Palestinian camps in Israeli attacks.

item.title: What’s happening at the World Cup one day before tournament starts?
item.description: Political issues continue to dominate the build-up, with the US-Israel war on Iran overshadowing the World Cup.

item.title: Why has Belfast erupted in anti-immigrant violence after a knife attack?
item.description: Ministers have blamed far-right online agitators for stoking racial tension following a stabbing.

item.title: Who pays for a supersized World Cup?
item.description: The biggest World Cup ever, with 48 teams and 104 matches, is expected to bring a record $13bn for FIFA.

item.title: Graham Platner wins Maine primary election: Results and key takeaways
item.description: Platner wins in Maine, Republican Nancy Mace loses in S Carolina. Results for Nevada and North Carolina also announced.

item.title: A four-year-old’s recovery from the trauma of war in Lebanon
item.description: Four-year-old Malika was seriously wounded in an Israeli attack that killed her mother.

item.title: Why is the FIFA World Cup 2026 being held in three countries?
item.description: The 2026 World Cup is the first to be held in three different host nations - but why? Al Jazeera explains.

item.title: Iran fires missiles at US bases carrying images of killed commanders
item.description: Iranian state media released video showing the launch of missiles towards US bases in the Middle East.

item.title: India’s Bengal pushes out Muslim Bangladeshis, deepening religious tensions
item.description: Hundreds 'pushed' to border and many others put in detention centres as part of a crackdown on undocumented migrants.

item.title: Argentina World Cup 2026 preview: Players to watch, group matches and squad
item.description: Messi is hoping to overcome injury to help Argentina become the first back-to-back World Cup champions since 1962.

item.title: Anti-immigration protests break out in Belfast after knife attack
item.description: Northern Ireland’s political leaders call for calm after a knife attack led to anti-immigration protests in the capital.

item.title: Rising from the rubble: A Gaza tech incubator defies Israel’s war
item.description: Israel's war on Gaza has decimated the tech sector, killing experts and destroying incubators that offered opportunity.

item.title: Spidercam crashes onto pitch during Hungary match
item.description: A spider camera crashed near the touchline during a football friendly between Hungary and Kazakhstan.

item.title: Twelve killed in Johannesburg mass shooting, manhunt under way
item.description: Police say a manhunt is under way for more than 10 suspects after the attack on an informal settlement.

item.title: Baltic states fear Russia-Ukraine war spillover after drone incursions
item.description: A spate of drone incursions fuels anxieties about a possible military conflict with Russia.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 explained in maps and charts
item.description: Qualified teams, groups, schedule and snapshot of stadiums that will host World Cup matches.

item.title: Explosion in Bahrain as Iran attacks US bases
item.description: CCTV footage shows an explosion in Bahrain’s capital Manama after what authorities say was an Iranian missile strike.

item.title: Messi comes off the bench to score in Argentina’s final World Cup warm-up
item.description: Argentina ease past Iceland in their final friendly before the World Cup, winning 3-0 in Auburn, Alabama.

item.title: Ukrainian drones strike Sevastopol museum and key Russian oil refineries
item.description: The multiday campaign has killed a rail worker, closed the bombed Chongar road bridge and caused a fuel crisis

item.title: Iran war day 103: US strikes after helicopter shot down, Tehran hits back
item.description: Analysts warn of further escalation after tit-for-tat attacks between Tehran and Washington.

item.title: Indonesia jails four military officers for acid attack on rights activist
item.description: Prosecutors argued in court that the soldiers were motivated by anger over Yunus’s activism.

item.title: Prediction platform Kalshi to collect job details to combat insider trading
item.description: Prediction platform says it will seek employment information for markets at 'heightened' risk of manipulation.

item.title: Platner wins Maine Democratic Senate primary, vows to oust Collins
item.description: Platner wins Maine Senate primary despite controversies, setting up a key race against Republican Susan Collins.
